params.name: Newcomen Engine
params.description:
On Jul 10, 1705 AD, Newcomen developed a new type of machine for mine drainage.
To help his partner solve the problem of mine drainage, Newcomen modified the old steam pump, separating the cylinder from the boiler to increase thermal efficiency, and using a piston device to enhance the conversion of firepower into mechanical force. This new type of pneumatic steam engine was quickly promoted and, decades later, became a reference prototype for Watt's improved steam engine.
